<!-- Generated by the 2026-07-19 multi-agent consolidation audit (13 agents, read-only).
     Repos audited: SaaSWrld/RocketPlate @ f33ab3d ("RP") and Dreone6/rocketplate-daily-delights @ HEAD ("DD"). -->

# 03 — UX & Engineering Grades

## Part 1 — UX Director audit

UX DIRECTOR AUDIT — RocketPlate portfolio (read-only; all claims verified against source files)

═══ DD — rocketplate-daily-delights ═══

**`/` (src/routes/index.tsx) — B+**
Strong first impression: night-gradient hero with a clear three-line value prop, ZIP-specific launch badge, three proof chips, and role-prefilled CTAs at every scroll depth; copy is confident and honest (partner names explicitly withheld until signed, Founding-100 terms restated in fine print). Motion discipline is genuinely excellent — reveal system, marquee, smooth scroll, and hero all gate on prefers-reduced-motion (styles.css:242-259, hooks). The conversion path is the weakness: every CTA opens a ~15-field modal (LeadForm.tsx) — heavy for cold traffic, phone is `required` in markup but optional in the Zod schema (LeadForm.tsx:123 vs leads.ts:63), and the duplicate path tells users "We updated your preferences" when the anon UPDATE silently affects zero rows under RLS (leads.ts:158-189) — a trust lie at the success moment. The share block hands out a cosmetic `/?ref=<firstname>` instead of the real referral code, and there are zero privacy/terms links anywhere despite collecting phone + SMS consent. Mobile: nav anchors vanish below `md` with no burger, leaving only the Join button — survivable for a one-pager, but lazy.

**`/company` (src/routes/company.tsx) — B**
The CinematicHero is the best-engineered hero in either repo (poster-as-LCP, staged entrance, 100svh no-CLS, reduced-motion honored), and the grouped three-audience FAQ, launch-map ZIP rows, and 5-step partner path give this page real information architecture. But it ships a literal placeholder: the header's only persistent CTA is a button labeled "Partner CTA" (company.tsx:101) — unforgivable on a brand flagship — and the primary nav CTA targets restaurant partners, not subscribers, muddling the page's conversion hierarchy. Footer is anchor-links-only: no legal, no contact, no address for a page whose JSON-LD claims to be the corporate home. Honest copy throughout ("Is RocketPlate live today? Not yet") is a genuine strength.

**DD overall: B** — Two beautifully art-directed pages with a documented motion system, undermined by shipped placeholder text, a lying success state, a fake referral mechanic, a modal-buried long form, and a complete absence of legal/consent scaffolding.

═══ RP — RocketPlate.Build ═══

**`/` (index.html) — A-**
The strongest page in the portfolio: distinct voice ("Dinner, cleared for launch", "SCROLL FOR LIFTOFF"), a scroll-choreographed 3D rocket that is desktop-only/idle-loaded/reduced-motion-pinned, and disciplined honesty (food photos disclaimed as representative at :878, dead-zone chart labeled illustrative, ZIP console shows real aggregate counts only). Conversion is smartly light — 4 fields + 2 consents with a progress bar and segment control (`aria-pressed`), the opposite of DD's modal wall — plus skip link, burger with `aria-expanded`/Escape, `role="status"` regions, and a sticky mobile CTA that hides over the form (:1423-1430). Deductions: user-facing success/fineprint links say "gorocketplate.com" but point at `*.netlify.app` (:1143, :1903), no favicon on this page, GTM is a dead placeholder so the whole funnel is unmeasured, mobile hero reserves `padding-bottom:46vh` of sky (:690) for a rocket that never loads on mobile, and the constructed $13.50→$26.92 receipt is a known open honesty item (LAUNCH-REVIEW #8).

**`/partners/` — B+**
Excellent B2B conversion architecture: ops-flavored "control card" hero, dead-zone bar chart, a 15-field application with inline validation (`aria-invalid` + focus-first-bad, :514-516), rate-limit-specific error copy, and — the standout — a tokenized 5-stage status pipeline that gives applicants a real "where am I" experience most pre-launch startups never build. But the page's own logo 404s: `../assets/logo.png` (:21, :200, :432) doesn't exist on the deployed site, so favicon plus header and footer brand images break on the single most credibility-sensitive page, and header/footer hardcode `rocketplate.netlify.app`. A broken logo on the page asking restaurateurs to trust you with their business caps this grade hard.

**`gorocketplate/` — A-**
A purpose-built conversion machine: single-CTA header, hero quick-capture that carries ZIP/email into the full form, ZIP checker with real neighbor counts, per-field `.ferr` errors, required-progress bar, optional fields tucked in a `<details>`, windowed sticky mobile CTA, complete `data-track` instrumentation, and a success state with account link, real server-issued referral code, share rail, and restaurant-referral micro-form. Reduced-motion and mobile breakpoints (960/600) are handled throughout; copy is punchy and honest ("Partner names are revealed at launch"). Deductions: 7 required field-groups including phone AND ≥1 cuisine is meaningful friction for a waitlist (and phone-required raises the TCPA stakes the legal pages haven't met yet); all footer/legal/partner links exit to `rocketplate.netlify.app`; the client still sends its own `launch_zone_status`/`referral_code` that the server overrides — harmless but sloppy; analytics events fire into a dead dataLayer.

**`/account` — B+**
A pre-launch product most competitors don't have: token-gated profile with prefilled preferences, zone/plan/F100 stat cards, save-then-refetch confirmation, referral link + live neighbor count, and a graceful no-token gate with a clear next step. Deductions: no way to revoke *email* consent or self-delete here even though the privacy page promises "update your preferences anytime via your private profile link" — SMS is toggleable, email is not; `SITE` and the back-links are `netlify.app`; a failed save gets one generic line with no retry context.

**`/admin` — B**
For an internal tool it's solid: token gate that self-clears on `unauthorized`, five stat tiles, four bar charts, four scrollable tables, CSV exports, everything HTML-escaped, session lock button. As UX: no loading state between unlock and render (the gate just sits there on slow RPCs), `alert()` for export failures, no sort/filter on the leads table, and the token-paste ritual is founder-hostile once a phone is involved. Acceptable for Phase Zero; would not survive a second operator.

**Legal pages (`/privacy`, `/terms`) + `/contact` — B-**
The plain-language, self-aware placeholder approach ("full formal policy publishes at public launch") is the honest way to do this, and both pages are readable, scoped, and styled consistently. But: both reference the nonexistent `../assets/logo.png` favicon, both are `noindex` yet listed in sitemap.xml (an internal contradiction), neither is linked adjacent to any consent checkbox where it matters, and the terms' Founding-100 clause is now the only legally-worded description of an offer three different pages describe slightly differently. Contact is an honest router page, same broken favicon.

**RP overall: A-** — A coherent seven-surface system with real end-to-end journeys (lead → account; application → status tracker), consistent honesty discipline, and strong accessibility signals, docked for the broken partner-page logo, pervasive `netlify.app` leakage in user-facing copy, a fully dead analytics layer, and placeholder legal beneath SMS-consent collection.

═══ Top 5 UX gaps in the consolidated future product ═══

1. **Consent without legal scaffolding.** DD collects phone + SMS consent with zero privacy/terms anywhere; RP's legal pages are self-declared placeholders not linked at any form's point of consent. Before paid traffic or a single SMS, ship real TCPA-reviewed policies and put the link inside every consent label on every form (LAUNCH-REVIEW open issue #7 confirms this is known and unresolved).

2. **Trust leaks at the pixel level: broken brand assets and `netlify.app` URLs.** The partners page — the highest-trust-required surface — renders with a 404'd logo and favicon (partners/index.html:21,200,432; same favicon bug on privacy/terms/contact), and success states, footers, and fineprint across both RP sites show or link `*.netlify.app` while the visible text claims the .com/.io domains. One asset-path fix plus the DNS cutover erases the single most visible credibility deficit.

3. **Three incompatible lead-capture philosophies.** Flagship: 4 fields (right). Go site: 7+ required groups including phone and cuisine. DD: ~15-field modal with a phone required/optional contradiction. The consolidated product should standardize on the flagship's light capture feeding the account page's progressive-profiling pattern — the enrichment form already exists at `/account`; stop front-loading it at the moment of least commitment.

4. **Referral/duplicate integrity.** The growth loop's currency is trust in the referral code, and the portfolio has three versions of it: DD shares a cosmetic first-name URL that isn't the DB code, the go site generates a client code the server overrides, and only `/account` shows the canonical one; meanwhile DD's duplicate path claims "updated your preferences" after a silently-failed RLS write. Consolidate on the server-issued code from `upsert_lead` everywhere, and never show success copy the backend can't back up.

5. **The funnel is flying blind.** GTM is a `GTM-XXXXXXX` placeholder in all three instrumented pages, so the meticulously built `rp_*` event dictionary (header→hero→zip→form→share) records nothing, and DD has no analytics at all — meaning the two-site variant test that justifies ADR-004 currently produces no comparable conversion data. Activating measurement is the highest-leverage single action in this entire audit: every other UX decision (form length, CTA copy, modal vs inline) is unfalsifiable until it lands.

Key files: `C:\Users\dreon\RocketPlate.Build\index.html`, `C:\Users\dreon\RocketPlate.Build\partners\index.html`, `C:\Users\dreon\RocketPlate.Build\gorocketplate\index.html`, `C:\Users\dreon\RocketPlate.Build\account\index.html`, `C:\Users\dreon\RocketPlate.Build\admin\index.html`, `C:\Users\dreon\RocketPlate.Build\privacy\index.html`, `C:\Users\dreon\RocketPlate.Build\terms\index.html`, DD routes/components under `...\scratchpad\rocketplate-daily-delights\src\`.

---

## Part 2 — Staff Engineer audit

# Engineering Quality Audit — DD vs RP

Read-only audit completed. Grades per dimension, evidence-backed, then overall + risk tables.

---

## DD — `rocketplate-daily-delights` (TanStack Start SSR, Lovable-built)

**Architecture fit-for-purpose: C-**
An SSR React meta-framework (TanStack Start + Nitro + React 19 + Tailwind 4) serving two static-content landing pages with one form. There is not a single `createServerFn` in the codebase — the SSR machinery (`src/server.ts`, `src/start.ts`, `src/integrations/supabase/client.server.ts`, `auth-middleware.ts`) is scaffolded and unused; all real work is client-side. The one thing SSR would justify (server-side lead validation/rate limiting) is exactly what's missing. This is a static site wearing a server costume. It also targets a **different Supabase project** (`bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc` in `DD/.env`) than production (`uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof`) — leads captured here land in a parallel database, an architectural split-brain at the business level.

**Code quality/consistency: B+**
The application code that exists is genuinely good: `LeadForm.tsx` (351 lines, Zod validation, live ZIP classification, back-compat `SEGMENT_TO_ROLE` mapping), SSR-safe hooks (`useReveal.ts`, `useSmoothScroll.ts` with reduced-motion + touch guards), `CinematicHero.tsx` with LCP-safe poster-first video. Deductions: dead code (`src/lib/waitlist.ts` + `waitlist_signups` table, unused `use-mobile.tsx`, unused server clients); a real bug in `src/lib/leads.ts:162` — the duplicate-email fallback issues an anon `UPDATE` on `leads` with **no anon UPDATE policy**, silently updating 0 rows while the UI claims "We updated your preferences"; cosmetic referral URL (`/?ref=<firstName>`) that doesn't use the DB `referral_code`; phone `required` in markup but optional in schema; package still named `tanstack_start_ts`.

**Dependency weight + supply chain: C**
50 runtime dependencies in `package.json`, of which recharts, embla-carousel, react-day-picker, vaul, cmdk, input-otp, react-resizable-panels, react-hook-form, date-fns are imported **only by the 46 untouched shadcn `ui/` boilerplate files** — zero app-code imports (grep-verified). That's the shadcn kitchen sink shipped for two pages and a dialog. Credit where due: `bun.lock` (1,409 lines) pins everything, and `bunfig.toml` has a real supply-chain control (`minimumReleaseAge = 86400`). Locked-but-obese.

**Build/deploy story: D**
No Netlify/Vercel/wrangler config anywhere; deployment is implicit Lovable Cloud (`.lovable/project.json`), with brand assets served as **CDN manifests** (`src/assets/*.asset.json` → `/__l5e/assets-v1/...` paths) that only resolve on Lovable hosting. The repo cannot be deployed anywhere else without asset surgery. `.env` with the anon key is **committed and not gitignored** — anon-key-only so not a leak, but it violates RP's rule 2 hygiene and normalizes a bad pattern. `AGENTS.md` warns the branch two-way syncs with the Lovable editor, i.e., the platform owns the git history.

**Performance: B**
SSR + code-split React is fine; ~615KB of committed JPGs in `src/assets` (`hot-meals-hero.jpg` 332KB is heavy for a hero-adjacent image). `HeroVideo.tsx` references `/assets/rocketplate-hero.{webm,mp4}` that don't exist in `public/` (only `favicon.ico`) — graceful SVG fallback, but dead fetch attempts on every load. Motion system is disciplined (Lenis lazy-imported, reduced-motion everywhere).

**Testing: F**
Zero test files (find-verified), no smoke script, no CI, no lint config beyond eslint in devDeps with no workflow running it.

**Documentation: C+**
`INTERACTION_DIRECTION.md` is excellent and the code follows it. But there is **no README**, no backend doc, no ADRs, no deploy doc — a new engineer cannot learn from the repo that it writes to a second Supabase project or how to ship it.

**Maintainability (1-person co): C-**
46 shadcn files + 50 deps + framework-version churn (Vite 8, nitro beta `3.0.260603-beta`, React 19, Tailwind 4 — all bleeding-edge) + Lovable wrapper (`@lovable.dev/vite-tanstack-config`) that owns the build config. When something breaks in that stack, a solo founder debugs five vendors. RP's zero-build approach has none of this surface.

**Constitution compliance: F**
- **RPC-only writes: VIOLATED** — `src/lib/leads.ts:155` direct anon `supabase.from("leads").insert(payload)`, no rate limiting, no captcha (RLS WITH CHECK constraints are good, but that's the pre-hardening RP architecture ADR-002 explicitly retired).
- **No secrets in git: bent** — anon key committed in `.env`, un-gitignored.
- **Migrations-only DDL: PASS** — 5 well-ordered migrations with genuinely strong RLS/CHECK discipline (this is DD's best backend work).
- **No fake data: PASS** — copy checked, no invented counters/testimonials.
- **Docs move with code: FAIL** — `waitlist.ts`/`waitlist_signups` dead path undocumented; 4 DB tables (`restaurant_partner_applications`, `email_events`, `user_roles`, `launch_zone_stats` view) with no consuming code and no doc explaining why.

---

## RP — `RocketPlate.Build` (zero-build static, 2 Netlify sites)

**Architecture fit-for-purpose: A**
For Phase Zero waitlist validation, static single-file pages + SECURITY DEFINER RPCs is close to the optimal architecture, and it's *deliberate*, with exit criteria: ADR-001 (static until Gate C), ADR-002 (RPC-only), ADR-003 (Netlify canonical), ADR-004 (two-site variant test into one `leads` table). Capability-token pattern for `/admin` and `/account` avoids building auth prematurely while keeping people-data unreadable publicly. 52 tracked files total; the whole company fits in one head.

**Code quality/consistency: B+**
Inline JS is disciplined: `esc()` HTML-escaping on all dynamic renders (admin:79, partners:441), aria/reduced-motion coverage throughout `index.html`, keyframe-table 3D choreography that's readable. Deductions: single-file pages are 95KB/68KB — heavy to edit safely without tests; copy/config duplication across the two sites is tolerated by ADR-001 but the client `ZONES` list is duplicated in 3 files and must manually match the DB `launch_zones` seed; client-supplied `referral_code`/`launch_zone_status` in the `upsert_lead` payload when the server computes its own; broken `../assets/logo.png` refs on partners/privacy/terms/contact (favicon + visible logo 404 on `/partners/` — lines 21/200/432); `noindex` pages listed in `sitemap.xml`.

**Dependency weight + supply chain: B-**
Zero npm deps, no lockfile, no build — great. But the flip side: GSAP 3.12.5, ScrollTrigger, Lenis 1.1.14, three.js r128 all load from cdnjs/jsdelivr with **zero `integrity=` attributes** (grep: 0 hits in both index files). A CDN compromise = arbitrary JS on pages that collect PII. Pinned versions mitigate; SRI hashes (or self-hosting under `/assets/` with immutable cache) would cost an hour and close the gap. Note three.js r128 is a 2021 release — old, but frozen-old is defensible here.

**Build/deploy story: A-**
`netlify.toml` per site with security headers (nosniff, X-Frame-Options DENY, Referrer/Permissions-Policy), immutable asset caching, server-side 301 for `/partner/*`. Git push → deploy, deploy-history rollback per ADR-003. Repo is pushed and current at `origin/master` (SaaSWrld/RocketPlate, verified via `git ls-remote`) — LAUNCH-REVIEW issue 5 ("GitHub backup blocked") appears **resolved in reality but stale in the doc**. Remaining: hardcoded `*.netlify.app` URLs pending DNS cutover, GTM placeholder.

**Performance: B+**
Actual sizes verified: flagship hero mp4 **16.6MB** but webm-first (1.6MB), `preload="none"`, HEAD-gated, desktop-only; go-site hero re-encoded to 5.7MB/0.38MB. Food images 105–125KB, poster 139KB, og 61KB. three.js (~600KB) injected on `requestIdleCallback`, desktop-only, reduced-motion pinned. `logo.png` at 182KB is the laziest asset (a PNG logo that should be a 5KB SVG, referenced on every page). Thoughtful budget overall.

**Testing: B**
`scripts/smoke.mjs` (88 lines, zero-dep) covers routes on both sites, the 301, security headers, immutable caching, a live RPC, and the edge-function self-test — and runs in CI (`.github/workflows/smoke.yml`) plus a Supabase keep-alive workflow. That's the right minimal harness for this architecture. Missing: no assertion on the write path (`upsert_lead` never exercised end-to-end), no HTML link-checker (would have caught the `assets/logo.png` 404s).

**Documentation: A-**
Best-in-class for a solo shop: constitution (`CLAUDE.md`), 4 real ADRs, `BACKEND.md`, `LAUNCH-REVIEW.md` as living QA truth, delta report with [V]/[I] evidence tags, DNS/analytics runbook, copy deck, motion spec. Deductions for verified drift: `BACKEND.md:134` lists 4 migrations vs ~10 actual; `gorocketplate/BUILD-NOTES.md:38-47` still describes the pre-hardening anon-INSERT architecture (contradicts ADR-002 and the code in the same directory); stale in-file comment `gorocketplate/index.html:826`; LAUNCH-REVIEW issue 5 stale. Rule 6 ("docs move with code") is violated by its own repo, mildly but measurably.

**Maintainability: B**
One person can hold all 52 files. Risks: 1,900-line single files with inline JS have no typechecking/linting, so regressions are invisible until smoke or manual QA; config triplication (ZONES, Supabase constants, copy) across `index.html`, `gorocketplate/index.html`, `partners/index.html` means every backend tweak is a 3-file find-and-replace. Acceptable until Gate C, and ADR-001 says exactly that.

**Constitution compliance: A-**
RPC-only: verified — every backend call in every page goes through `/rest/v1/rpc/` (index 1823/1860, partners 451, admin 83, account 97, smoke 39); zero direct table writes. No secrets: only the publishable key in HTML. Migrations-only DDL: consistent with docs. No fake data: disclaimers present ("dishes representative," receipt labeled illustrative — though issue 8 flags the constructed $13.50→$26.92 figures as needing real day-of-launch numbers). Docs-move-with-code: the one soft failure (drift above).

---

## Overall grades

| Repo | Overall | One-line verdict |
|---|---|---|
| **RP** (`C:/Users/dreon/RocketPlate.Build`) | **A-** | Deliberate, documented, testable, constitution-honoring architecture for its phase; drift in docs and missing SRI keep it off A. |
| **DD** (scratchpad `rocketplate-daily-delights`) | **C-** | Polished frontend craft on top of the wrong architecture for a 1-person company: unused SSR framework, 50 deps, no tests, no README, platform lock-in, and a write path that violates ADR-002 against a second, disconnected database. |

The stack comparison is decisive: DD's TanStack Start SSR + React 19 + Tailwind 4 + bun buys nothing this business currently uses (no server functions, no dynamic data, no auth) while adding a five-vendor debug surface, Lovable hosting lock-in, and framework churn a solo founder must service. RP's zero-build static approach trades DRY-ness for total ownership and near-zero operational load — the correct trade until Gate C, and it's written down as ADR-001 with an exit trigger. DD's only clear wins over RP: TypeScript + Zod at the form layer, a lockfile with a supply-chain age gate, and componentized copy.

## Top engineering risks

**DD**
| # | Risk | Evidence | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Split-brain backend: leads go to Supabase `bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc`, not production `uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof` — captured demand is invisible to the real funnel | `DD/.env`, `supabase/config.toml` | Critical (business) |
| 2 | Direct anon INSERT on `leads`, no rate limiting/captcha; violates ADR-002 | `src/lib/leads.ts:155` | High |
| 3 | Silent-failure duplicate path: anon UPDATE with no UPDATE policy affects 0 rows, UI reports success | `src/lib/leads.ts:162` + migration 4 policies | High |
| 4 | Platform lock-in: Lovable CDN asset manifests + no deploy config = repo not portable | `src/assets/*.asset.json`, no netlify/vercel/wrangler files | High |
| 5 | Zero tests, zero CI, no README | find/glob verified | Medium-High |
| 6 | ~41 unused runtime deps + beta nitro + Vite 8 churn on a 2-page site | `package.json`, grep of app imports | Medium |
| 7 | Committed un-gitignored `.env`; dead `waitlist.ts` path and 4 orphaned DB tables | `.env`, `src/lib/waitlist.ts`, migrations 1 & 4 | Medium |

**RP**
| # | Risk | Evidence | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | No SRI on 4 CDN scripts loaded on PII-collecting pages | 0 `integrity=` hits in `index.html`, `gorocketplate/index.html` | High |
| 2 | Doc drift vs code: BACKEND.md migration list (4 of ~10), BUILD-NOTES pre-hardening DB claims, stale in-file comment | `BACKEND.md:134`, `gorocketplate/BUILD-NOTES.md:38-47`, `gorocketplate/index.html:826` | Medium |
| 3 | Broken `../assets/logo.png` → visible 404 logo/favicon on `/partners/`, privacy, terms, contact | `partners/index.html:21,200,432` etc. | Medium |
| 4 | Triplicated client config (ZONES, Supabase constants) must manually track DB seed; client-sent `referral_code`/`launch_zone_status` | `index.html:1220`, `gorocketplate/index.html:1120-1124` | Medium |
| 5 | No typecheck/lint/tests over ~5,000 lines of inline JS; smoke never exercises the write path | `scripts/smoke.mjs:80-81` (read-only RPC + selftest only) | Medium |
| 6 | Admin token pasted in chat 2026-07-07 still unrotated; GTM placeholder; `*.netlify.app` URLs pre-DNS | LAUNCH-REVIEW checklist, delta §0.5-G | Medium (founder-gated) |
| 7 | Stale LAUNCH-REVIEW issue 5: repo IS pushed to github.com/SaaSWrld/RocketPlate (verified) — doc claims backup blocked | `git ls-remote` vs LAUNCH-REVIEW.md | Low (doc fix) |

## Part 3 — Brand & Product audit

# Brand + Product Audit: DD (rocketplate-daily-delights) vs RP (RocketPlate.Build)

Audited read-only, 2026-07-19. Reference system: RP `COPY-DECK.md` (voice + copy bank + banned phrases) and `DESIGN-DIRECTIONS.md` (approved "Warm Launch" hybrid).

---

## Dimension 1 — Brand consistency vs the approved system

### Palette: DD is a faithful port (strongest area)
DD's `src/styles.css` (lines 7–12, 54–94) reproduces the Warm Launch palette exactly — Navy `#1C3A6E`, Crimson `#C0182A`, Comfort Orange `#F28C28`, Steam Cream `#FFF8EE`, Soft Steel `#BECCD6` — matching RP `index.html:39–46` token-for-token (as oklch conversions with hex comments). Cream-dominant surfaces, sunset gradient (crimson→orange), navy contained in bands/cards, grain utility, steam/flame keyframes: the "supper club that runs like mission control" temperature is respected. Minor additions: a `--rocket` blue `#0B5E8F` not in RP's flagship tokens, and DD's `/company` hero is a full-viewport navy photo hero — defensible as the approved hero-film treatment, but it pushes navy outside "contained cards."

### Type: DD drops two-thirds of the approved stack
Approved (D1, implemented in RP): **Fraunces display / Outfit body / IBM Plex Mono telemetry**. DD uses **Fraunces + Inter** (`styles.css:50–51`) and has zero mono usage in app code (only a shadcn chart boilerplate hit). The mono telemetry layer is a load-bearing Warm Launch ingredient — "Mono labels operational facts only (times, ZIPs, counts); the serif owns everything human" (DESIGN-DIRECTIONS.md:58–61). DD's ZIP console, timeline clocks, and telemetry moments all render in Inter. Deviation, not interpretation.

### Rocket usage
RP flagship: procedural WebGL rocket with orbiting plate, scroll-keyframed flight across 13 sections — the D1 "page spine." DD: a charming 2D SVG rocket (`RocketPlate.tsx`) with hover/launch animation on `/`, and a photo hero on `/company` whose poster follows the approved hero-film art direction (silver rocket, hull slogan). DD's rocket is a mascot moment, not a spine. Acceptable secondary usage; not the flagship expression.

### Voice + banned phrases: DD has hard violations
Grep results against COPY-DECK.md:7–9 bans:

| Violation | Location | Rule broken |
|---|---|---|
| "Not DoorDash. Not meal prep. Something better." — as the How-it-works H2 | DD `src/routes/index.tsx:281` | Names a competitor AND uses "meal prep"; the deck's assigned H2 is "One system. Three moving parts." |
| FAQ: "Is this DoorDash or Uber Eats?" | DD `src/routes/index.tsx:705` | Deck explicitly assigns "Is this just another delivery app?" — "(no competitor names)" (COPY-DECK.md:24) |
| FAQ: "Is this a meal kit or chilled meal prep?" | DD `src/routes/index.tsx:709` | Borderline — "meal prep" used as contrast, not description; the ban's spirit is contrast-adjacent. Flag, low severity |
| Constructed fee figures: "$14 meal becomes $28" (pain card), "$4.99+ / 10–15%" (NoFees table) | DD `index.tsx:79, 420–421` | Same class as RP's own open issue #8 ($13.50→$26.92 receipt) — illustrative numbers unlabeled. DD and RP also disagree with each other ($14→$28 vs $13.50→$26.92), so the brand tells two different fee stories |
| Founding offer drift: "33% off week 5–8" | DD `index.tsx:408` | Deck says "33% off month two"; DD `/company:201` gets it right — DD is internally inconsistent |

Clean on the worst bans: no fake restaurant names (TrustStrip is cuisine types only), no fake testimonials, no fake counters, and admirably honest launch status ("Is RocketPlate live today? Not yet." — `company.tsx:510`). Pricing matches ($84.99/$124.99, 5/7 meals).

### Message-slot misassignment
DD `/` correctly carries the conversion H1 ("Hot local meals. One weekly subscription. Zero delivery chaos."). But DD `/company` — the rocketplate.io persona — leads with "Hot local meals. Zero delivery chaos." (`CinematicHero.tsx:108`), a remix of the *conversion* H1. The approved brand-site H1 is "Dinner, cleared for launch." / "Hot local meals, delivered daily." DD collapses the deliberate brand-vs-conversion voice split that ADR-004's variant test depends on.

**Verdict D1: RP.** RP is the system; DD is a ~85% faithful palette/temperature port with a missing type layer, a diluted rocket, misassigned H1s, and two flat banned-phrase violations that would fail the deck's own QA.

---

## Dimension 2 — Product completeness vs Phase Zero (validate Miami demand)

| Capability | RP | DD | Winner |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Waitlist conversion** | Quick capture + full form + ZIP checker with real counts, real referral codes + account tokens returned by `upsert_lead`, email queue, rate limiting, GTM-ready `rp_*` event dictionary, A/B via `landing_page_variant` | Excellent form UX (role-conditional fields, live ZIP classifier, Zod) but: writes to a **different Supabase project** (`bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc` vs production `uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof`) so leads land in a severed database; direct anon INSERT violating ADR-002; no rate limiting; duplicate "update" silently affects 0 rows while claiming success; referral link is cosmetic (`?ref=<firstName>` — unattributable); zero analytics events | **RP**, decisively |
| **Partner acquisition** | Dedicated `/partners/` product: 15-field application via RPC, tokenized status tracker, 5-stage pipeline UI, dead-zone economics pitch | Marketing sections only; `restaurant_partner_applications` table exists in DD's DB with **no form writing to it**; restaurants funnel into generic leads | **RP**, decisively |
| **Ops visibility** | Admin dashboard (totals, zones, roles, cuisines, variants, CSV exports), account self-service, email-event queue + edge worker, smoke tests + CI keepalive | Admin RLS machinery in the DB (`user_roles`, `has_role`, `launch_zone_stats`) with **no admin UI**; the stats view is broken for anon anyway | **RP**, decisively |

**Verdict D2: RP on all three counts.** DD is two polished landing pages; RP is a small operating system. For Phase Zero specifically, every DD signup today would be a demand signal lost to a database nothing reads.

---

## Dimension 3 — Message architecture overlap

The two repos run the *same* two-persona architecture (conversion site + brand site), so nearly every section exists twice:

| Slot | RP wording | DD wording | Winner |
|---|---|---|---|
| Conversion H1 | "Hot local meals. One weekly subscription. Zero delivery chaos." | Identical | Tie (DD complied) |
| Brand H1 | "Dinner, cleared for launch." | "Hot local meals. Zero delivery chaos." | **RP** — distinctive, ownable, and the assigned slot; DD's contaminates the variant test |
| How-it-works H2 | "One system. Three moving parts." | "Not DoorDash. Not meal prep. Something better." | **RP** (DD's is punchier but banned; a compliant rewrite would be "Not another delivery app. Not a meal kit.") |
| FAQ lead | "Is this just another delivery app?" | "Is this DoorDash or Uber Eats?" | **RP** (deck-mandated) |
| Founding 100 offer | "first week free · 33% off month two · 12-month rate lock" | `/company` matches; `/` says "week 5–8" | **RP** phrasing as canonical |
| Fee-pain proof | Animated receipt $13.50 → $26.92 | "$14 becomes $28" + comparison table | **RP's receipt** for theater, but pick ONE canonical illustrative figure brand-wide (RP open issue #8 already covers replacing it with day-of-launch reality) |
| Step titles | "Restaurants cook / RocketPlate batches / Subscribers eat hot" | "Pick your plan / Kitchens batch-cook / One hot evening drop" | **DD** — more concrete and benefit-forward; harvest-worthy |

**DD lines worth harvesting into COPY-DECK's reserve bank** (on-voice, currently better than RP's equivalents):
- "We own the kitchen relationships, the delivery experience, and the food temperature at your door." (`index.tsx:284`)
- "The Founding 100 are the neighbors who unlock RocketPlate in their zone." (`company.tsx:385`)
- "Waitlist signals drive the schedule." (`company.tsx:510`)
- "Fresh during their slow afternoon window — never reheated leftovers." (`index.tsx:268`)
- The honest status FAQ pattern ("Is RocketPlate live today? Not yet.")

DD also carries two message surfaces RP has only as form segments: dedicated **ForDrivers** and **ForInvestors** sections. Worth considering for RP only if driver/investor lead volume warrants it — in Phase Zero it's scope, not signal.

**Verdict D3: RP's architecture and most wordings win; DD contributes 5–6 harvestable lines and better step titles.** A third live property would confound ADR-004's clean two-variant experiment.

---

## Dimension 4 — What each genuinely has that the other lacks

### DD has, RP lacks
1. **React/TypeScript componentization with a Zod schema as validation source of truth** (`lib/leads.ts`) — RP duplicates validation logic in inline JS per page. Relevant at Gate C per ADR-001, not before.
2. **Role-conditional progressive form** (customer-only plan/household/cuisine fields appear on select) — a real conversion-UX improvement over RP go-site's show-everything form.
3. **Unique photography**: `chef-kitchen.jpg` and `doorstep-delivery.jpg` (committed) + the "silver rocket at sunset" cinematic hero poster (Lovable CDN only — must be downloaded to be portable). RP has more total real assets (4 food photos + shipped hero film), but these three lifestyle/brand images don't exist in RP.
4. **SSR per-route heads via TanStack Start** — mostly moot: RP's static HTML is already fully server-rendered SEO-wise, and RP has JSON-LD where DD's `/` does not.
5. **Gate-C schema patterns**: `user_roles` + `has_role()` RBAC, `launch_zones` table driving UI, referral-code trigger — good reference designs, wrong database.
6. Radix/shadcn accessibility primitives and toast feedback.

### RP has, DD lacks
1. The **entire real backend**: RPC-only writes, IP rate limiting, email queue + Resend edge worker, account self-service, admin dashboard + CSV exports, capability tokens, smoke tests, CI keepalive — all against the production Supabase project.
2. The **partner application product** with status tracking.
3. **Analytics**: full `rp_*` dataLayer dictionary, GTM-ready, DNS/analytics runbook. DD tracks nothing.
4. **Shipped hero film assets** (webm+mp4+poster, both sites). DD's video slots reference files that don't exist.
5. The **signature three.js rocket** — the most ownable brand expression in either repo.
6. **Governance**: constitution, 4 ADRs, copy deck, design directions, launch review, delta report, legal routes, security headers.

### DD liabilities (disqualifying for production as-is)
Committed `.env`; parallel Supabase project splitting the lead funnel; ADR-002 violation; broken duplicate-update path; cosmetic referral codes; Lovable-CDN-hosted logo/poster; no deploy config; no analytics; banned-phrase copy.

**Verdict D4: complementary, not competitive.** DD's genuine surplus is a handful of assets, form-UX patterns, and copy lines — not a platform advantage. RP's surplus is the entire validated business machine.

---

## Final recommendation — Brand source of truth

**RP (RocketPlate.Build) is and should remain the sole brand source of truth**: `COPY-DECK.md` for voice/copy, `DESIGN-DIRECTIONS.md` ("Warm Launch") for visuals, the shipped flagship + go sites as reference implementations, production Supabase `uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof` as the only lead database.

**Treat DD as a quarry, then retire it from the live path:**
1. **Do not deploy DD** under any RocketPlate domain in its current state — banned-phrase copy, severed backend, ADR-002 violation, and a third variant that would contaminate the ADR-004 experiment.
2. **Harvest** (small, high-value): the 5–6 copy lines and step titles listed in D3 into COPY-DECK's reserve bank; the role-conditional form pattern as a go-site enhancement idea (Phase 2 backlog); download `chef-kitchen.jpg`, `doorstep-delivery.jpg`, and the cinematic hero poster from Lovable before the project lapses.
3. **Bank for Gate C**: DD's Zod schema, RBAC pattern, and TanStack Start setup are useful references for the framework adoption ADR-001 already schedules — evaluated then, not adopted now.
4. If DD's Supabase project (`bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc`) has captured any real leads, export and merge them into production `leads` before decommissioning; otherwise pause/delete the project to close the split-funnel and committed-anon-key exposure.

Per-dimension score: Brand consistency — RP. Phase Zero completeness — RP (3/3 sub-areas). Message architecture — RP wins the system, DD contributes lines. Unique value — complementary, RP structurally ahead. Overall: RP 4/4.
